title: About
text:
Hi, I’m Maria and I’m a PhD student at CASA UCL in the City Modelling Lab.
I’m studying the social and spatial inequalities in transport, especially in the context of the UK’s EV transition and I have a particular focus on car dependence. I have a particularly strong (negative) feeling towards SUVs in cities. In former lives I played classical flute, got my master’s in physics and worked as an energy consultant in a Big Company. In current and parallel lives I’m a (very) amateur forager, nature enthusiast, vegan, and climate activist. Woof.

title: 1: Road rage ain’t just for cars
Would Maria do that?
text:
Yes, Maria Wood.
Welcome to my informal and poorly maintained blog where I, Maria, occasionally write about things I care about including cars and transport, climate, politics, life, etc. Sometimes the takes are hot, sometimes they are quite tepid. It’s a temperature roulette in here.
I promise that the aesthetics of this site are in progress. But it’s what’s on the inside that counts, right?
Check out the blog here

title: An ode to the SUV
text:
i luv driving in my suv it’s my Cinderella carriage it’s so vast and huge and wonderful it won’t fit in my garage
i luv driving in my suv i’m off-road all the time from kensington to richmond park the bus is just a crime
I luv driving in my suv i’m way up high, so tall there’s kiddies playing in the way? can’t seem them, they’re too small!
i luv driving in my suv spewing clouds of co2 cant find an XL parking space i’ll have to straddle two
i luv driving in my suv its size is unimpeded perfect for these city streets where more [tank-sized cars are/ person-crushing’s/fatal strikes are] needed
i luv driving in my suv it makes me look so wealthy! its called “conspicuous consumption” it’s shows my self esteem is healthy
i luv driving in my suv i honk at cyclists loads! though please ignore the fuel efficiency or rate of wear to roads
i luv driving in my suv i’d even drive electric so long as i can cruise and kill and keep my city streets dyspeptic
